verbatim_text: Also, Gemini apparently displays contradictory beliefs depending on how you prompt the model. So in the above one, Gemini says no to Isajai River located south of Salar del Arazaro. But then when you ask it, Is Salar del Arazaro located south of the Isajai River, it also says no. OK, so there's a lot more examples of these failures, but they make it hard to trust these neural network systems to act autonomously in real world high stakes scenarios. So how can we address these failures? So the solution really depends on how we interpret these failures, both descriptively and normatively. So the first view is that these large neural networks are essentially bags of heuristics. And of course, this is certainly partly true. But if this were the whole story, then our only solution would be to scale our training data. There's no principled way to understand how these systems failed or to guarantee that we've actually fixed their errors.
